impact: This gives the daemon a deterministic recovery rule after repeated SyntaxError, py_compile, TS1005, TS1109, or TS1128 failures: shrink the next proposal, compile syntax-bearing files first, and inspect existing accepted contracts before adding a new parallel implementation. That should reduce rollback loops like public_frontier_checkpoint by steering workers toward one parser-clean file or a tiny fixture/test pair.
summary: Tighten supervisor repair guidance for repeated parser rollbacks and require reuse of existing committed contracts before similarly named modules are proposed.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 4 recent validation rollbacks were syntax or compile failures; patch daemon prompt, preflight, or repair guidance before more worker attempts

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
